content:
# Perf v3 Quality Analysis — dorea grade (2026-04-03)

## Methodology
Compared interpolated output (default settings, temporal interp ON) vs full-pipeline reference (`--no-depth-interp`, same commit 135f8df). Both runs auto-calibrated independently, introducing minor CUDA non-determinism in calibration values.

## Test File
- **Input**: `footage/raw/2025-11-01/DJI_20251101111428_0055_D.MP4`
- **Resolution**: 3840x2160 @ 119.88fps, 1671 frames

## Aggregate Metrics (interpolated vs full-pipeline reference)

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| **PSNR (avg)** | 32.02 dB |
| **PSNR (min)** | 22.57 dB (frame 1671 — trailing edge) |
| **PSNR (max)** | inf (frame 1 — identical keyframe) |
| **SSIM (All)** | 0.907 |
| **SSIM (R/G/B)** | 0.843 / 0.935 / 0.943 |

## PSNR Distribution (1671 frames)
```
22-26 dB:    3 frames  (0.2%) — trailing frames at clip end
26-29 dB:   23 frames  (1.4%) — near scene cuts / rapid motion
29-33 dB:  284 frames (17.0%) — moderate interpolation delta
33-37 dB:  905 frames (54.2%) — bulk of frames, good quality
37-40 dB:  213 frames (12.7%) — near-keyframe, excellent
40-44 dB:  128 frames  (7.7%) — keyframes + neighbors
inf:         1 frame   (0.1%) — frame 1 (first keyframe, identical)
```

## Key Observations
1. **Red channel weakest**: SSIM R=0.843 vs G=0.935, B=0.943. Red is most affected by depth-aware correction, so interpolation error is amplified in that channel.
2. **Trailing frames worst**: Frames 1669-1671 (22-26 dB) are the last frames of the clip where the lookahead buffer runs out and only the preceding keyframe is available. Known design limitation.
3. **Only 1 identical frame**: Despite keyframes running the full pipeline in both modes, CUDA non-determinism in auto-calibration causes slight differences across all frames. The delta from calibration jitter is ~42 dB on keyframes.
4. **97% of frames above 29 dB**: The vast majority of frames are in the "good to excellent" range for video quality.

## Visual Assessment
- **Mid-clip frames** (t=2s, 5s, 10s): No visible interpolation artifacts. Color correction, depth separation, and detail recovery all look correct.
- **Worst-case frame** (1656, ~26 dB): Visible tone/contrast shift compared to reference — warmer, slightly brighter, less foreground detail. Still looks like a properly graded frame; not "broken."
- **No ghosting or banding** visible on any sampled frame.

## Verdict
Quality is acceptable for the 10.7x speedup. The interpolation trades pixel-exactness for temporal coherence — at 120fps with max_interval=12, non-keyframe frames are perceptually close to their fully-graded equivalents. The trailing-frame issue (last 3 frames) is a minor edge case solvable with a trailing keyframe flush.

## Reference Run Timing (--no-depth-interp, same commit)
- **Wall time**: 18m 9.5s (vs 3m 33s with interpolation, vs 38m baseline)
- **Throughput**: ~1.5 fps (all frames run full pipeline with perf v3 IPC/LAB/HF optimizations)
- The other perf v3 optimizations (raw RGB IPC, fused LAB, HF bypass) provide a **2.1x speedup** independently of temporal interpolation.
